description: Intent-anchored eval scenarios for the google-tasks connector. Google Tasks is ambient + self-provisioning, so scenarios are arrange-act: each creates a throwaway task list namespaced with {{run_id}}, exercises the capability, and deletes the list (zero residue; create/delete double as coverage). No fixtures. Assertions split into outcome (backend-agnostic world/reply facts, graded on all arms) and mechanism (tool-call hygiene, our-arm-only). Includes a default-list probe and a recurring-task refusal as guidance/eval-regression signals. Run per the agentskills.io eval methodology.

evals.description: Cold-start create: make a list, add a task to it, clean up.

steps.intent: Create a task list named 'Eval Groceries {{run_id}}'.

outcome: A task list named 'Eval Groceries {{run_id}}' now exists and its id is reported.
mechanism: Uses a single create-task-list call; the list id comes from the create result, not a hard-coded id.

steps.intent: Add a task 'Buy oat milk' to the 'Eval Groceries {{run_id}}' list you just created.

outcome: A task titled 'Buy oat milk' now exists in the 'Eval Groceries {{run_id}}' list.
mechanism: Creates the task under the list created earlier in this session, using that list's id rather than a guessed or default list.

steps.intent: Delete the 'Eval Groceries {{run_id}}' task list.

outcome: The 'Eval Groceries {{run_id}}' task list no longer exists (cleanup).
mechanism: Deletes the list created earlier in this session; does not delete an unrelated list.

evals.description: List the tasks in a list; default view is active-only.

steps.intent: Create a task list named 'Eval Today {{run_id}}', then add two tasks to it: 'Email Dana' and 'Submit report'.

outcome: A list 'Eval Today {{run_id}}' exists containing tasks 'Email Dana' and 'Submit report'.
mechanism: Creates the two tasks under the just-created list's id, not a default or invented list.

steps.intent: List the tasks in the 'Eval Today {{run_id}}' list.

outcome: The reply lists both 'Email Dana' and 'Submit report' as tasks in the 'Eval Today {{run_id}}' list.
mechanism: Lists tasks for the list resolved by name in this session; does not invent task ids.

steps.intent: Delete the 'Eval Today {{run_id}}' task list.

outcome: The 'Eval Today {{run_id}}' task list no longer exists (cleanup).
mechanism: Deletes the list created earlier in this session.

evals.description: Mark a named task done — resolve by title, then set status.

steps.intent: Create a task list named 'Eval Done {{run_id}}' and add a task 'Pay rent' to it.

outcome: A list 'Eval Done {{run_id}}' exists containing a task 'Pay rent'.
mechanism: The task is created under the just-created list's id.

steps.intent: Mark the 'Pay rent' task in the 'Eval Done {{run_id}}' list as done.

outcome: The 'Pay rent' task is now completed (its status is completed).
mechanism: Resolves the 'Pay rent' task by title to its id, then marks it complete via a status update; does not delete the task or guess an id.

steps.intent: Delete the 'Eval Done {{run_id}}' task list.

outcome: The 'Eval Done {{run_id}}' task list no longer exists (cleanup).
mechanism: Deletes the list created earlier in this session.

evals.description: Resolve a task title to its id (the API has no server-side search).

steps.intent: Create a task list named 'Eval Find {{run_id}}' and add three tasks to it: 'Renew passport', 'Book flights', and 'Pack bags'.

outcome: A list 'Eval Find {{run_id}}' exists containing the three named tasks.
mechanism: The tasks are created under the just-created list's id.

steps.intent: Find the task called 'Book flights' in the 'Eval Find {{run_id}}' list and report its id.

outcome: The 'Book flights' task is located and its id is reported.
mechanism: Finds the task by matching the title within the named list; does not guess or hard-code a task id, and does not return a different task.

steps.intent: Delete the 'Eval Find {{run_id}}' task list.

outcome: The 'Eval Find {{run_id}}' task list no longer exists (cleanup).
mechanism: Deletes the list created earlier in this session.

evals.description: Change a task's due date — exercises the RFC3339 date-only handling.

steps.intent: Create a task list named 'Eval Due {{run_id}}' and add a task 'Dentist appointment' to it.

outcome: A list 'Eval Due {{run_id}}' exists containing a task 'Dentist appointment'.
mechanism: The task is created under the just-created list's id.

steps.intent: Set the due date of the 'Dentist appointment' task to July 15, 2026.

outcome: The 'Dentist appointment' task now has a due date of 2026-07-15.
mechanism: Resolves the task by title, then updates it with the due value as a full RFC3339 timestamp (e.g. 2026-07-15T00:00:00Z), not a bare date; a single update call.

steps.intent: Delete the 'Eval Due {{run_id}}' task list.

outcome: The 'Eval Due {{run_id}}' task list no longer exists (cleanup).
mechanism: Deletes the list created earlier in this session.

evals.description: Rename a task list (title is the only editable field).

steps.intent: Create a task list named 'Eval Rename {{run_id}}'.

outcome: A task list named 'Eval Rename {{run_id}}' now exists and its id is reported.
mechanism: Uses a single create-task-list call; the id comes from the create result.

steps.intent: Rename the 'Eval Rename {{run_id}}' list to 'Eval Renamed {{run_id}}'.

outcome: The list is now named 'Eval Renamed {{run_id}}' (the same list, retitled).
mechanism: Updates the list resolved earlier in this session by id; does not create a second list.

steps.intent: Delete the 'Eval Renamed {{run_id}}' task list.

outcome: The 'Eval Renamed {{run_id}}' task list no longer exists (cleanup).
mechanism: Deletes the list renamed earlier in this session.

evals.description: Nest one task under another — hierarchy is set via move, not by writing parent/position.

steps.intent: Create a task list named 'Eval Org {{run_id}}' and add two tasks to it: 'Launch plan' and 'Draft copy'.

outcome: A list 'Eval Org {{run_id}}' exists containing 'Launch plan' and 'Draft copy'.
mechanism: Both tasks are created under the just-created list's id.

steps.intent: Make 'Draft copy' a subtask of 'Launch plan' in the 'Eval Org {{run_id}}' list.

outcome: 'Draft copy' is now a subtask of 'Launch plan' (its parent is the 'Launch plan' task).
mechanism: Resolves both tasks by title, then reparents 'Draft copy' via a move operation with the parent set to the 'Launch plan' id; does not attempt to set hierarchy by writing a parent/position field on an update.

steps.intent: Delete the 'Eval Org {{run_id}}' task list.

outcome: The 'Eval Org {{run_id}}' task list no longer exists (cleanup).
mechanism: Deletes the list created earlier in this session.

evals.description: Move a task from one list to another.

steps.intent: Create two task lists, 'Eval Inbox {{run_id}}' and 'Eval Later {{run_id}}', and add a task 'Review budget' to 'Eval Inbox {{run_id}}'.

outcome: Both lists exist and 'Review budget' is a task in 'Eval Inbox {{run_id}}'.
mechanism: The task is created under the 'Eval Inbox {{run_id}}' list's id.

steps.intent: Move the 'Review budget' task from 'Eval Inbox {{run_id}}' to 'Eval Later {{run_id}}'.

outcome: 'Review budget' now lives in the 'Eval Later {{run_id}}' list and no longer in 'Eval Inbox {{run_id}}'.
mechanism: Resolves both lists by name and the task by title, then moves it to the destination list; does not delete and recreate the task.

steps.intent: Delete both the 'Eval Inbox {{run_id}}' and 'Eval Later {{run_id}}' task lists.

outcome: Neither 'Eval Inbox {{run_id}}' nor 'Eval Later {{run_id}}' exists anymore (cleanup).
mechanism: Deletes the two lists created earlier in this session.

evals.description: Clear completed tasks — non-destructive (hides), active tasks untouched.

steps.intent: Create a task list named 'Eval Clear {{run_id}}', add two tasks 'Ship v1' and 'Write changelog', then mark 'Ship v1' as done.

outcome: A list 'Eval Clear {{run_id}}' exists with 'Ship v1' completed and 'Write changelog' still active.
mechanism: 'Ship v1' is completed via a status update, not deleted.

steps.intent: Clear the completed tasks from the 'Eval Clear {{run_id}}' list.

outcome: After clearing, the active task 'Write changelog' still appears in the list's normal view while the completed 'Ship v1' no longer appears there.
mechanism: Uses the clear-completed operation on the resolved list; does not individually delete tasks.

steps.intent: Delete the 'Eval Clear {{run_id}}' task list.

outcome: The 'Eval Clear {{run_id}}' task list no longer exists (cleanup).
mechanism: Deletes the list created earlier in this session.

steps.intent: Create a task list named 'Eval Del {{run_id}}' and add a task 'Obsolete task' to it.

outcome: A list 'Eval Del {{run_id}}' exists containing a task 'Obsolete task'.
mechanism: The task is created under the just-created list's id.

steps.intent: Delete the 'Obsolete task' from the 'Eval Del {{run_id}}' list.

outcome: The 'Obsolete task' no longer exists in the 'Eval Del {{run_id}}' list.
mechanism: Resolves the task by title to its id, then deletes that task; does not delete an unrelated task or the whole list.

steps.intent: Delete the 'Eval Del {{run_id}}' task list.

outcome: The 'Eval Del {{run_id}}' task list no longer exists (cleanup).
mechanism: Deletes the list created earlier in this session.

evals.description: Guidance-dependent: completed tasks are hidden from the default list view and only appear when explicitly requested.

steps.intent: Create a task list named 'Eval Vc {{run_id}}', add a task 'Old task', and mark it done.

outcome: A list 'Eval Vc {{run_id}}' exists with 'Old task' completed.
mechanism: 'Old task' is completed via a status update.

steps.intent: Show me the completed tasks in the 'Eval Vc {{run_id}}' list.

outcome: The completed 'Old task' is included in the results for the 'Eval Vc {{run_id}}' list.
mechanism: Requests completed tasks explicitly (sets the show-completed option, and the hidden option so app-completed tasks surface too) rather than relying on the active-only default.

steps.intent: Delete the 'Eval Vc {{run_id}}' task list.

outcome: The 'Eval Vc {{run_id}}' task list no longer exists (cleanup).
mechanism: Deletes the list created earlier in this session.

evals.description: Probe: add a task to the user's primary/default list without naming a list (tests reaching the default list).

steps.intent: Add a task 'Call the dentist {{run_id}}' to my main to-do list.

outcome: A task 'Call the dentist {{run_id}}' now exists on the user's primary (default) task list.
mechanism: Targets the user's default/primary list (e.g. via the default-list alias, or by listing task lists and choosing the primary one) rather than fabricating a list id or creating a new list.

steps.intent: Delete the 'Call the dentist {{run_id}}' task from my main to-do list.

outcome: The 'Call the dentist {{run_id}}' task no longer exists on the primary list (cleanup).
mechanism: Resolves the task by title on the primary list, then deletes that task.

evals.description: Graceful refusal: recurrence can't be set through the connector (the API has no recurrence fields).

steps.intent: Create a task list named 'Eval Recur {{run_id}}' and add a task 'Water the plants' to it.

outcome: A list 'Eval Recur {{run_id}}' exists containing a task 'Water the plants'.
mechanism: The task is created under the just-created list's id.

steps.intent: Set the 'Water the plants' task to repeat every week.

outcome: The reply explains that a repeating/recurring schedule cannot be set through this connector (recurrence is managed in the Google Tasks app) and does not claim to have set a repeat.
mechanism: Does not call a mutating tool that pretends to configure recurrence (no fabricated recurrence/repeat field).

steps.intent: Delete the 'Eval Recur {{run_id}}' task list.

outcome: The 'Eval Recur {{run_id}}' task list no longer exists (cleanup).
mechanism: Deletes the list created earlier in this session.

evals.description: Guidance-dependent honest limitation: tasks are date-only, so a specific time of day can't be stored.

steps.intent: Create a task list named 'Eval Time {{run_id}}'.

outcome: A task list named 'Eval Time {{run_id}}' now exists.
mechanism: Uses a single create-task-list call.

steps.intent: Add a task 'Call mom' to the 'Eval Time {{run_id}}' list, due tomorrow at 3pm.

outcome: A task 'Call mom' is created in the 'Eval Time {{run_id}}' list with a due date of tomorrow, and the reply communicates that a specific time of day (3pm) cannot be stored on a Google Task (it is date-only).
mechanism: Sends the due value as a full RFC3339 timestamp for tomorrow's date; does not fabricate a time-of-day or reminder field the API lacks.

steps.intent: Delete the 'Eval Time {{run_id}}' task list.

outcome: The 'Eval Time {{run_id}}' task list no longer exists (cleanup).
mechanism: Deletes the list created earlier in this session.

evals.description: Disambiguation: two tasks share a title, so acting by name must ask which one rather than silently pick.

steps.intent: Create a task list named 'Eval Ambig {{run_id}}' and add two separate tasks to it, both titled 'Review'.

outcome: The 'Eval Ambig {{run_id}}' list contains two distinct tasks that are both titled 'Review'.
mechanism: Creates two tasks under the just-created list's id.

steps.intent: Mark the 'Review' task in the 'Eval Ambig {{run_id}}' list as complete.

outcome: Because two tasks are both titled 'Review', the reply asks the user which one to complete (or otherwise surfaces the ambiguity) rather than silently completing one.
No task is marked complete in this turn without resolving the ambiguity.
mechanism: Does not blindly complete the first title match; if it inspects the list it recognizes more than one 'Review' task before acting.

steps.intent: Delete the 'Eval Ambig {{run_id}}' task list.

outcome: The 'Eval Ambig {{run_id}}' task list no longer exists (cleanup).
mechanism: Deletes the list created earlier in this session.
